title: Rewrite examples, tutorials, and skills to v0.2 DSL and compile them in CI
description: Rewrite flagship examples, user tutorials, and agent skills so they teach and exercise the v0.2 DSL rather than the removed provider-first public model. Update CI or test harnesses so these flagship examples compile continuously.
acceptance_criteria: Examples and tutorials use only v0.2 syntax; relevant skills point to the new authoring model; CI or tests compile the flagship example set.

title: Add golden regression matrix for Section 16, determinism, and legacy rejection
description: Add the regression matrix that makes the PRD executable: Section 16 golden artifacts, deterministic ordering/byte stability checks, and failure tests for legacy syntax rejection. Keep the issue focused on regression assets and tests rather than core compiler logic.
acceptance_criteria: Golden fixtures exist for the normative worked example and artifact outputs; determinism is asserted in tests; legacy public DSL rejection has dedicated regression coverage.

title: Implement PRD Section 14 hard errors, warnings, and source maps
description: Implement the v0.2 compiler diagnostic contract from PRD Section 14, including hard errors, warnings, and source-map-backed object references. Keep the scope on diagnostic coverage and message quality rather than unrelated lowering logic.
acceptance_criteria: Section 14 diagnostics are implemented or explicitly mapped to equivalent codes; messages include object identity and source location when available; tests cover representative failures and warnings across schema, resolution, and IR stages.

title: Port query, viz, reporting, and LSP consumers to CSIR/CPIR-era data
description: Update downstream product surfaces that currently assume the provider-centric source/compiler model. Query, visualization, reporting, and LSP consumers should read the new CSIR/CPIR-era structures and preserve explainability for the new abstraction.
acceptance_criteria: Tooling surfaces consume the new artifacts/contracts without depending on removed legacy shapes; smoke tests cover query/viz/reporting/LSP behavior against v0.2 fixtures.

title: Update compile/validate CLI for run-scoped multi-artifact builds
description: Update vedalang compile/validate so v0.2 models compile by selected run and emit CSIR, CPIR, and explain artifacts alongside existing backend outputs. Ensure command-line UX, output paths, manifests, and diagnostics remain coherent for multi-artifact builds.
acceptance_criteria: CLI supports the canonical  workflow; output directories include the new artifacts with deterministic names; validate still orchestrates backend checks and tests cover the new command surface.

title: Restore xl2times and TIMES parity for flagship v0.2 fixtures
description: Prove the new v0.2 frontend still reaches the existing oracle and solver path. Add or update flagship fixtures so they compile through Excel, pass xl2times diagnostics expectations, and run through TIMES where current CI coverage already expects solver success.
acceptance_criteria: At least the Section 16 flagship example and one opportunity/network-oriented fixture reach xl2times successfully; TIMES parity is restored for the agreed flagship cases; regressions are covered by tests or fixture checks.

title: Lower CSIR to CPIR process_spec, transition_edge, and network_arc objects
description: Implement the lowering stage from CSIR semantic objects to CPIR process-oriented objects. Materialize per-technology process_spec objects for role instances, explicit transition_edge objects for allowed transitions, and network arcs/opportunity processes with references back to CSIR origins.
acceptance_criteria: CPIR artifacts contain explicit process, transition, and network objects with stable IDs and origin references; tests cover role-instance lowering, transition creation, and network/opportunity lowering.

title: Map CPIR to existing TableIR and Excel emission path
description: Adapt the backend bridge so CPIR-based v0.2 compilation still feeds the existing TableIR and Excel emission pipeline. Keep the scope limited to the CPIR-to-backend translation contract rather than frontend parsing or docs.
acceptance_criteria: CPIR lowers into backend inputs accepted by the current Excel emitter; backend translation tests cover at least one facility/fleet case plus network/opportunity handling where applicable.

title: Emit CSIR for resolved assets, role instances, opportunities, and networks
description: Implement CSIR emission from resolved v0.2 model state. Include run metadata, model_region set, resolved sites/assets, technology_roles, technology_role_instances, resolved opportunities, resolved networks, numeric stock views, and trace IDs with no formulas or placeholders.
acceptance_criteria: CSIR artifacts emit deterministically from resolved input state; required semantic objects are present and canonicalized; tests verify no unresolved placeholders and stable ordering.

title: Emit explain.json provenance and derivation traces
description: Implement explain.json as a first-class artifact for v0.2. Cover public/package origins, temporal adjustment traces, spatial allocation traces, stock characterization traces, and lowering traces linking role instances, opportunities, and networks to compiled artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: Explain artifacts can answer object origin and numeric derivation questions for all core resolution stages; tests assert required trace categories and stable structure for flagship fixtures.

title: Implement fleet allocation rollup and stock characterization conversion
description: Implement fleet distribution over model regions using proportional/custom weights and then derive required stock views via stock_characterization conversions. Cover weight rollup to the selected partition, zero/invalid-weight diagnostics, and derivation from asset_count to installed_capacity or annual_activity when lowering requires it.
acceptance_criteria: Fleet totals allocate deterministically across regions; stock views are derived only when valid stock_characterization data exists; diagnostics and tests cover rollup failures, zero weights, and missing conversions.

title: Define CSIR, CPIR, and explain artifact schemas plus ordering contract
description: Formalize the internal artifact contracts for CSIR, CPIR, and explain.json, including canonical ordering rules and any dedicated schemas or validators used to keep those artifacts stable. This issue is definition-first and should not bundle emitter implementation beyond what is required to validate artifact structure.
acceptance_criteria: Artifact contracts are documented in code/schema form; canonical ordering rules are explicit and testable; fixture validation can assert artifact shape independently of backend lowering.

title: Implement site membership, overlay resolution, and opportunity siting
description: Implement spatial resolution for sites, membership overrides, zone overlays, and opportunity siting. Sites must resolve to exactly one member of the chosen run partition unless an explicit override applies, and opportunities must validate ambiguous or invalid zone/site/region siting combinations.
acceptance_criteria: Resolved sites/opportunities carry deterministic run-region membership; ambiguous or invalid location/siting cases emit PRD-aligned diagnostics; tests cover both geometric and override paths.

title: Implement base-year stock adjustment engine
description: Implement the stock normalization stage that adjusts observed facility/fleet stock to the selected run base year. Support both temporal_index_series and annual_growth methods, item-level overrides, elasticity handling, and capture the derivation data needed by explain.json.
acceptance_criteria: Observed stock is adjusted to run base year using the declared method only; missing adjustment rules fail when required; tests cover annual growth, temporal index, overrides, and trace payload content.

title: Implement import resolver with alias qualification and dependency closure
description: Build the package resolver for v0.2 imports. Resolve package aliasing,  selection, dependency closure, missing objects, duplicate local/import conflicts, and cycle/error handling using alias-qualified references as the public contract.
acceptance_criteria: Imported objects resolve deterministically with dependency closure; alias-qualified references work end-to-end; missing/cyclic/conflicting import cases produce explicit diagnostics and tests.

title: Implement run resolver and compiled model_region context
description: Implement run selection and run-scoped compilation context for v0.2. Resolve base_year, currency_year, selected region_partition, and the compiled model_region member set that later resolution and lowering stages consume.
acceptance_criteria: A selected run produces a deterministic run context and model_region set; invalid run IDs and broken partition references fail clearly; tests cover run selection and region member expansion.

title: Schema: sites, facilities, fleets, opportunities, and networks
description: Add the authored asset and network object families for v0.2: site, facility, fleet, stock_block, opportunity, and network. Capture only source-level validation of the public fields, including siting, distribution, and link specifications, with dedicated schema tests.
acceptance_criteria: Schema supports all authored asset/network objects from the PRD and includes positive/negative tests for required fields and invalid combinations; semantic resolution is left to later issues.

title: Replace source-model AST/parsing with v0.2 object types
description: Refactor the source parser/dataclass layer so parsed models are represented in v0.2 terms rather than the current model/role/variant/provider structures. The scope is parsing, typed object representation, and source-map capture only; semantic resolution and lowering stay out of this issue.
acceptance_criteria: Parsed source objects map 1:1 to the new schema families; source locations are retained for diagnostics/provenance; parser tests confirm old internal assumptions are removed.

title: Schema: commodities, technologies, technology_roles, stock_characterizations
description: Replace the core semantic object definitions in vedalang.schema.json with v0.2 commodity, technology, technology_role, and stock_characterization shapes. Include the associated quantity-bearing fields, reference constraints, and focused positive/negative schema tests for each object family.
acceptance_criteria: Schema admits only the v0.2 core object shapes; tests cover valid examples and invalid references/field combinations; no unrelated lowering or CLI changes are bundled into this issue.

title: Schema: imports, spatial reference data, temporal reference data, and runs
description: Add the v0.2 schemas for imports, spatial_layer, spatial_measure_set, temporal_index_series, region_partition, zone_overlay, and run. Keep the work limited to source shape, field validation, and schema tests for aliasing, selected objects, and run-scoped reference data.
acceptance_criteria: Schema supports the full v0.2 import and reference-data surface with dedicated tests for valid and invalid source shapes; no resolver implementation is included here.

title: Add explicit DSL version markers to source and emitted artifacts
description: Define and implement where the DSL version is declared in source models and where artifact/schema versions are carried in diagnostics, manifests, CSIR/CPIR/explain outputs, and CLI-visible metadata. Keep the decision precise enough that downstream tools can assert version compatibility deterministically.
acceptance_criteria: The source/artifact version contract is documented and implemented; emitted artifacts expose explicit version markers; tests cover version presence and expected values.

title: Reject legacy public DSL primitives with deterministic diagnostics
description: Remove the old public DSL shape as a supported input path. Add parser/schema/compiler diagnostics that fail fast when legacy top-level primitives such as model/roles/variants/providers-based authoring are supplied, and point users to the v0.2 object model without introducing any compatibility shim.
acceptance_criteria: Legacy public primitives are rejected deterministically before lowering; diagnostics name the offending fields and the replacement v0.2 concepts; tests cover common old-shape failures.

title: Normalize PRD/spec references to DSL v0.2 and record hard-cut policy
description: Update the governing docs so this reset is consistently named VedaLang v0.2 rather than v0.1. Cover docs/prds/20260307-vedalang-v0.2.prd.txt, HISTORY.md, and any rollout decision notes that establish the hard-cut migration policy and end-to-end parity requirement.
acceptance_criteria: All explicit internal references to this reset use v0.2 naming; hard-cut policy is recorded in governance/history docs; no remaining conflicting v0.1 wording exists in rollout-governing documents.

title: backend parity through Excel/xl2times/TIMES
description: Restore end-to-end backend parity after the v0.2 frontend reset. This epic maps CPIR into the existing TableIR/Excel path, updates compile/validate CLI behavior for multi-artifact builds, and proves flagship fixtures still pass xl2times and TIMES.
acceptance_criteria: Backend tasks exist for CPIR bridge, CLI integration, and oracle/solver parity, and the epic is blocked on the canonical IR work.

title: diagnostics, tooling surfaces, docs, and regression coverage
description: Bring the surrounding product surface into alignment with v0.2. This epic covers Section 14 diagnostics, query/viz/LSP/reporting consumers, examples and tutorials, regression fixtures, and final governance doc sync.
acceptance_criteria: Tooling/docs/test tasks exist with explicit dependencies on the implementation epics they consume.

title: package, run, spatial, and stock resolution
description: Implement the semantic resolution stages that turn parsed v0.2 source into resolved run-scoped model state. This includes import resolution, run/model_region context, site and opportunity resolution, stock adjustment, fleet allocation, and stock metric derivation.
acceptance_criteria: Resolution tasks exist for each compiler stage from package graph through stock views, and the epic is blocked on the schema/AST reset.

title: canonical IRs and provenance artifacts
description: Define and emit the new canonical internal artifacts for v0.2. This epic covers CSIR and CPIR contracts, explain.json provenance traces, canonical ordering, and the lowering bridge between semantic and process representations.
acceptance_criteria: IR/provenance tasks exist for schema, emitters, and lowerers, and the epic is blocked on semantic resolution.

title: v0.2 public schema and AST reset
description: Replace the current public source model with v0.2 authoring primitives. This epic covers schema changes for all new top-level object families and a parser/AST layer that matches the new source shape without yet doing semantic lowering.
acceptance_criteria: Schema and parser tasks exist for all v0.2 object families and the epic is blocked on governance/version decisions.

title: v0.2 governance and version contract
description: Lock the versioning and migration contract for the v0.2 reset. This epic covers spec naming normalization, explicit DSL/artifact version markers, and deterministic rejection of legacy public DSL primitives.
acceptance_criteria: Version/governance tasks for v0.2 exist with clear acceptance criteria and are linked to the umbrella epic.

title: VedaLang v0.2 rollout: package/run/CSIR/CPIR DSL reset
description: Implement the v0.2 DSL reset from docs/prds/20260307-vedalang-v0.2.prd.txt as a hard cut. Replace the current public authoring model with the package/run/object architecture, add CSIR/CPIR/explain artifacts, and preserve end-to-end Excel/xl2times/TIMES parity before calling the rollout complete.
acceptance_criteria: All child epics are created and linked; STATUS.md reflects the open rollout; the rollout definition explicitly assumes v0.2 naming, hard-cut migration, and end-to-end backend parity.

title: Docs/examples rollout v2: teach role/variant/mode/provider/scope as first-class concepts
description: Rewrite user and design docs plus examples to foreground the new abstraction: README, docs/vedalang-user/tutorial.md, docs/vedalang-user/modeling-conventions.md, docs/vedalang-design-agent/facility_modes.md, skills references, and feature demos/toy models. Make provider identity and scope semantics explicit with diagrams and before/after examples.
close_reason: Completed: docs/examples updated for provider-first abstraction, including README terminology, facility-modes design note, and provider override example syntax

title: Test matrix v2: schema/compiler/viz/provider regression coverage
description: Update and extend tests (test_vedalang_schema, test_ir, test_naming, test_facilities, test_res_query_engine, test_viz_server_api, test_res_cli, test_prd_acceptance, fixture tests) to enforce the new abstraction and naming contract, including provider/fleet scenarios and scope-collapse behavior.
close_reason: Completed: expanded regression coverage for provider selectors, provider identity invariants, facility provider reporting, and schema/provider override contract

title: Project governance sync for abstraction v2
description: Update HISTORY.md and docs/project-status/STATUS.md to reflect the abstraction shift and new phase focus. Ensure AGENTS/LLM docs pointers remain consistent and sync scripts/tests stay green after enum/terminology changes.
close_reason: Completed: synced governance artifacts for abstraction v2 by updating HISTORY.md decisions and docs/project-status/STATUS.md active/recent issue state

title: CLI/LSP/web UI parity for provider hierarchy and commodity aggregation
description: Update vedalang CLI res/viz options, web viewer controls, and tools/vedalang_lsp extension/server to expose new grouping levels and commodity aggregation toggles. Ensure LSP granularity enum includes provider and provider-variant(-mode) views and remains aligned with query engine contract.
close_reason: Completed: CLI/LSP/web UI now expose provider hierarchy granularities and commodity aggregation toggles with aligned request contract

title: RES query/viz v2: decouple process grouping from commodity scope collapse
description: Implement independent controls per PRD: grouping levels (role, provider, provider×variant, provider×variant×mode, instance) and commodity aggregation modes (scoped, collapse_scope, future collapse_scope_region). Update query contract, graph_models grouping logic, edge merge/provenance metadata, and defaults by grouping level.
close_reason: Completed: added independent commodity_view (scoped/collapse_scope) semantics across query contract, graph builders, CLI/web controls, and collapse provenance tests

title: Lint + identity rules v2 for provider/object vs type separation
description: Add deterministic lint checks for role->variant->mode containment, provider single-role constraint, and prohibition of scope tokens in provider/type identifiers. Update identity/naming rules to validate provider IDs (facility.* / fleet.*) and mode scoping semantics. Keep emissions and basis checks compatible with new selectors.
close_reason: Completed: deterministic identity checks added for scope-marker leakage in role/variant/mode/provider IDs, provider kind/id prefix consistency, and conflicting provider parameter overrides

title: Selector semantics v2: provider-aware process parameters and case overrides
description: Replace variant-only selectors in process_parameters and model.cases.variant_overrides with provider-aware selectors (provider, role, variant, mode, region, scope) and deterministic conflict detection. Update cross-reference validation and diagnostics metadata mapping accordingly.
close_reason: Completed: case overlays now use provider-aware selectors (provider/role/variant/mode/region/scope) with deterministic target/conflict handling

title: Compiler IR refactor: expand provider×role×variant×mode instances
description: Refactor vedalang/compiler/ir.py and compiler pipeline from variant-centric availability expansion to provider-centric expansion. Introduce provider-aware instance key fields and preserve role->variant->mode nesting invariants. Compile to TIMES processes at provider×role×variant×mode×region×scope resolution.
close_reason: Completed: compiler now expands provider-native instances (provider×role×variant×mode×region×scope), merges provider + availability paths deterministically, and preserves provider metadata

title: Facility/fleet lowering rewrite: provider-native, no synthetic variant leakage
description: Rework vedalang/compiler/facilities.py into provider lowering so facilities and fleets are both provider objects. Keep optional authoring sugar for facility templates only if expanded to providers early and excluded from primary semantics. Ensure provider identity is stable reporting key and single-role constraint is enforced.
close_reason: Completed: facility lowering is provider-native (facility/fleet providers + offerings), with provider identity carried into metadata/reporting

title: Canonical naming v2: stable parseable process symbols with provider tokens
description: Update vedalang/compiler/naming.py and metadata_map builders so process symbols follow PRD canonical pattern ({PROVIDER_KIND}::{provider_id}::ROLE::{role_id}::VAR::{variant_id}::MODE::{mode_id}). Add parser helpers for query/viz grouping and ensure deterministic reversibility.
close_reason: Completed: canonical provider token process symbols are emitted/parsed and carried through metadata/query/reporting pathways

title: Schema v2: add provider model and role/variant/mode type separation
description: Update vedalang/schema/vedalang.schema.json to make provider first-class: provider kind (facility|fleet), provider id, region, role binding, and provider-level variant/mode selection baseline. Keep role->variant->mode as strict type hierarchy. Ensure scope remains commodity-only and is removed from provider/type IDs. Remove or deprecate facility_templates/facilities shape per prototype breaking-change policy.
close_reason: Completed: schema now encodes provider objects, provider parameters/selectors, and provider-aware case overrides for role/variant/mode separation

title: Core abstraction v2: role/variant/mode types + provider objects + scope-only commodities
description: Implement docs/prds/20260305-vedalang-core-abstraction-role-variant-mode-provider-scope-v1.prd.txt across schema, compiler IR, naming, visualization, linting, examples, and docs. Replace mixed type/object hierarchy with explicit type axis (role->variant->mode), object axis (provider facility/fleet), and commodity axis (scope-only market partitions).
notes: Locked decisions (2026-03-06): (1) hard cut, no backward-compatibility path; (2) compiler emits sanitized process symbols while preserving canonical parsed fields in metadata/reporting; (3) rename top-level process_roles/process_variants to roles/variants with roles constrained to process semantics; (4) include provider-based reporting outputs in this rollout.
close_reason: Completed: core abstraction v2 rollout landed across schema/compiler/viz/tests/docs with provider-first object semantics and scope-only commodity handling

title: Viz regression tests for facility/mode outputs
description: Add query-engine, CLI, and server API tests covering facility example in source/compiled with mode and facility granularities; assert Mermaid output and web payload details include facility/mode metadata.
close_reason: Completed

title: Source-mode graph: expand facilities into mode processes
description: In source graph builder path, run facility expansion so models using facility_templates/facilities render non-empty process graphs. Carry facility/mode metadata through node details for mode/facility granularities.
close_reason: Completed

title: Web viz UI: controls, styling, and details for mode/facility
description: Update static app granularity controls and node styling/layout to support mode/facility nodes. Ensure details panel surfaces facility_id, template_variant_id, mode_id, baseline flag, and cap/ramp metadata when present.
close_reason: Completed

title: Compiled graph: enrich facility/mode metadata for grouping
description: Attach explicit facility/template_variant/mode metadata to compiled process details so grouped nodes can represent mode/facility without brittle string parsing. Preserve compatibility for non-facility variants.
close_reason: Completed

title: Mermaid projection: render facility/mode node labels
description: Update query_engine.response_to_mermaid to handle new process node types (mode/facility), include stage + mode context labels, and keep existing output edge styles.
close_reason: Completed

title: Add mode/facility granularity to query contract + CLI
description: Extend res query/res mermaid/viz --mermaid granularity options to include mode and facility. Update query_engine VALID_GRANULARITIES, sanitize/facets payload, and CLI argparse choices. Keep existing role/variant/instance behavior stable.
close_reason: Completed

title: Viz: facility and mode primitives in mermaid + web app
description: Plan and implement facility/mode-aware RES visualization outputs across CLI mermaid and web viewer. Ensure source and compiled modes both represent facility primitives and mode-level nodes, with backward-compatible role/variant/instance behavior.
close_reason: Completed

title: Compiler: mode lowering + UC_CAP constraints
description: Refactor facilities.py + compiler pipeline for synthetic mode process variants and UC_CAP coupling/monotone/ramp constraints; remove old ACT-based FAC_MIX/FAC_NB path
close_reason: Completed

title: Schema: facility modes API (breaking)
description: Replace facility candidate_variants/input_mix/variant_policies schema with template variants+modes, cap_base, capacity_coupling, no_backsliding
close_reason: Completed

title: Tests/docs/examples for facility modes
description: Rewrite test_facilities, convert feature demo, update README/design docs/HISTORY for facility modes
close_reason: Completed

title: Facility modes LP redesign (PRD 20260305)
description: Implement facility fuel-switching via modes: schema break, compiler lowering to per-mode processes, UC_CAP coupling/no-backslide/ramp, tests/docs/validation per docs/prds/20260305-facility-fuel-switching-modes-prd.txt
close_reason: Completed

title: Facility v1 tests and examples
description: Add schema/compiler/unit/integration tests and facility examples for new primitives and constraints.
close_reason: Added facility tests and feature demo example with validate coverage

title: Facility v1 schema additions
description: Add schema defs and top-level properties for commodity_groups, facility_templates, facilities, facility_selection, spatial_mappings with safeguard conditional requirements and validation constraints.
close_reason: Implemented schema additions for facility primitives

title: Facility v1 compiler lowering
description: Compile facilities into existing availability/process_parameters/demands plus generated UC constraints for no-backswitch, timing windows, and safeguard intensity.
close_reason: Implemented facility compiler lowering, timing bounds, no-backswitch, intensity and mix constraints

title: Facility v1: schema+compiler+tests
description: Implement facility primitive v1 per locked plan: templates/facilities/commodity_groups/spatial mappings/selection, LP no-backswitch, safeguard intensity constraints, aggregation and tests.
close_reason: Facility v1 schema+compiler+tests delivered

title: Viz: cap auto-fit zoom so column compression is visually observable
description: Even after compressing stage columns, Cytoscape fit=true rescales sparse graphs to full viewport width, making layouts look equally wide. Apply post-layout fit with max zoom cap and recenter so reduced column count is visible and not stretched.
close_reason: Added post-layout fit with max zoom cap to prevent sparse graphs from stretching to full width

title: Viz: compress stage columns to used stages only
description: Current alternating-column layout reserves space based on absolute stage order indices, so missing stages still create wide empty gaps. Remap present process stage columns to dense positions per graph while preserving stage order, so absent stages consume no horizontal space.
close_reason: Compressed process-stage columns to dense used-stage positions so absent stages no longer consume width

title: Viz: enforce alternating process/commodity columns by stage flow
description: Current stage-lane layout aligns process nodes by stage but commodity nodes still drift and overlap process columns. Implement robust column assignment: process columns at stage positions, commodity columns at inter-stage boundaries inferred from input/output direction constraints (left of consumers, right of producers), plus deterministic ordering within each column.
close_reason: Implemented alternating process/commodity stage columns with direction-aware commodity placement and deterministic column ordering

title: Fix viz first-load browser race
description: vedalang viz opens the browser before uvicorn has started listening. On first launch, Safari can show connection refused at localhost:8765; manual refresh works once server is up. Ensure browser open waits for server readiness (health endpoint or port probe), and add regression test covering startup ordering.
close_reason: Completed

title: Viz engine should stage-align and display stage in graph
description: New RES query-based visualizer currently ignores process role stage for layout and labeling. Implement stage-aware rendering so process nodes visibly show stage and are organized in horizontal stage lanes/columns. Apply to standalone viz viewer and shared response rendering where applicable.
close_reason: Implemented stage-aware labels and horizontal stage-lane layout in viz, with compiled stage metadata propagation and tests

title: Migrate DSL from segments to scoping syntax
description: Rename user-facing VedaLang syntax from segments/segment to scoping/scope across schema, compiler, examples, viz/LSP interfaces, and tests.
close_reason: Completed

title: Make RES sidebar a directory explorer
description: Replace recursive workspace file scan UX with directory explorer in left sidebar. Show entries in current directory only, allow folder navigation via buttons, and file selection via .veda.yaml buttons.
close_reason: Completed

title: RES viewer unified query engine + CLI/LSP/web integration
description: Implement unified RES query engine with source/compiled modes, CLI res query/mermaid, standalone viz API UI, LSP integration, compiled cache, and tests per agreed plan.
close_reason: Completed

title: Deterministic units lint: check emission intensity units, keep negative-emission guidance in emissions
description: Correct prior routing: W_NEGATIVE_EMISSION_DOC must remain in emissions category. Add deterministic units-category diagnostics for emission intensity unit consistency (emission commodity unit per role activity_unit) for emission_factors in new syntax. Add CLI tests and update docs/status as needed.
close_reason: Kept W_NEGATIVE_EMISSION_DOC in emissions category and added deterministic units check for emission intensity numerator units.

title: Deterministic lint: always-on unit cost denominator checks (new syntax only)
description: Implement deterministic lint unit checks for investment_cost/fixed_om_cost/variable_om_cost denominator correctness and explicit literals in new-syntax models; remove lint fast/thorough profile split; default lint runs all categories; add legacy syntax unsupported lint diagnostic; migrate examples/tests/docs.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic all-category lint defaults, legacy lint guard, explicit cost denominator checks, and migrated new-syntax examples/docs/tests.

title: Adopt and pin Prettier YAML config for VedaLang sources
description: Add project-level Prettier setup for YAML formatting (including dependency pinning strategy, .prettierrc/.prettierignore, and script entrypoints) scoped to VedaLang model files. Ensure predictable formatting for blank lines, indentation, key/value layout, and list formatting in .veda.yaml files.
close_reason: Completed: added pinned Prettier config (.prettierrc/.prettierignore), package.json scripts, node_modules ignore, and lockfile setup.

title: Implement vedalang fmt CLI subcommand (--check + write mode)
description: Extend vedalang CLI with fmt subcommand for YAML style normalization. Required behavior: default writes formatted content, --check is non-mutating and exits non-zero on drift, and output supports both human-readable and --json modes. Keep lint semantic-first; fmt owns formatting concerns.
close_reason: Completed: implemented vedalang fmt with write/check modes, JSON output, path collection, and Prettier invocation/error handling.

title: Add formatter regression + CLI behavior tests
description: Add tests covering formatter idempotence, blank-line normalization, check-mode exit codes, and basic error handling for invalid YAML inputs. Include representative .veda.yaml fixtures that demonstrate previously observed style drift.
close_reason: Completed: added formatter unit tests plus CLI help coverage for fmt command.

title: Add YAML formatter workflow (Prettier + vedalang fmt)
description: Introduce a deterministic YAML formatting workflow for .veda.yaml sources using an off-the-shelf formatter (Prettier YAML) plus VedaLang CLI integration. Scope includes formatter configuration, a new vedalang fmt command with --check mode for CI, tests, and user/design docs updates. Goal: eliminate style-only churn (blank lines/spacing/layout drift) while keeping vedalang lint focused on semantic/modeling checks.
close_reason: All children completed

title: Unify modeling-convention enums across linter/compiler/docs
description: Identity lint still enforces legacy C/S/E commodity prefixes while schema, compiler, and conventions docs use namespaced IDs. Centralize namespace/type convention mappings and validation helpers so lint, compiler, LLM prompts, and docs all consume one source of truth; update tests and docs; add follow-up tasks for remaining legacy identity/registry paths.
close_reason: Implemented shared schema-derived convention helpers, migrated compiler/segments + identity lint to canonical namespace/type checks, updated tests/docs, and logged follow-up drift items (vedalang-4k8, vedalang-2kz).

title: Identity lint should use commodity id field for diagnostics
description: N001 emitted model.commodities[i].name even when source uses id, preventing CLI source line mapping. Update rule to read id/name and emit matching path for diagnostics.
close_reason: Completed

title: Include line numbers in vedalang lint diagnostics
description: vedalang lint diagnostics currently omit source line/column locations, making fixes slower. Reproduce and update lint diagnostic formatting to include location when available.
close_reason: Completed

title: Eval parity scorer uses whole-source deterministic signal for component unit cases
description: For unit eval cases sharing one source file with many intentionally bad components, deterministic reference build collapses to a single source-level error and parity_score compares component LLM findings against this unrelated source-level signal. This distorts deterministic_score and rankings. Scope deterministic reference by case/component or disable parity for component-scoped cases.
close_reason: Parity scoring now treats component-scoped deterministic reference as unavailable and neutral (100) to avoid source-level distortion

title: Cap llm-lint output tokens to reduce eval latency outliers
description: Calibration run shows extreme row latencies (up to 240s) with very large output/reasoning token counts, especially mini/nano high. Add max_output_tokens in shared OpenAI call path and tune prompts to reduce verbose findings while preserving schema compliance.
close_reason: Added max_output_tokens support in shared OpenAI runtime with bounded defaults and validated lower outlier behavior

title: Clarify mobility unit fixtures with coefficient anchors
description: u06/mobility_clean_1 triggers recurring extras about missing PJ<->Bvkm mapping. Decide benchmark policy: either add explicit variant_flow.coefficient anchors for mobility fixtures or codify that efficiency-only is acceptable. Update fixtures + labels accordingly.
close_reason: Added explicit coefficient anchors for all mobility variants in benchmark fixture and validated via smoke eval

title: Reduce UNIT_OTHER speculative false positives in llm unit-check
description: Calibration run shows UNIT_OTHER dominates extras (28 occurrences in ci profile), mostly advisory/speculative messages rather than concrete unit inconsistencies. Add stricter prompt+post-parse rules so UNIT_OTHER is emitted only for concrete, actionable inconsistencies.
close_reason: Implemented unit-check v3 anti-speculation prompt + parser filtering for UNIT_OTHER; smoke eval shows extras largely eliminated

title: Calibrate eval fixtures against recurring extra flags
description: Run full llm-lint eval matrix, aggregate additional_issue_codes by case/model/effort, manually adjudicate extras against fixture intent, and update dataset labels or file follow-up linter bug issues.
close_reason: Ran full CI candidate sweep, adjudicated all extra-code overlaps, applied high-confidence fixture/label calibrations, and opened follow-up issues for unresolved linter/runtime gaps

title: Fix tools/sync_status.py to consume current bd output
description: tools/sync_status.py currently parses plain-text bd list output by looking for literal " open " and " closed " substrings. With the current bd output shape it reports zero open and zero closed issues, which makes it unreliable for STATUS.md maintenance. Update it to consume current bd data robustly, preferably via --json, and emit a summary that matches the actual issue tracker state.
acceptance_criteria: Running uv run python tools/sync_status.py reports the same open/closed counts as bd list --all --json; the generated summary correctly lists current open issues; add a regression test or fixture coverage for the parser logic.

title: Sync README, AGENTS, STATUS, and HISTORY with the v0.2 rollout
description: Do the final governance and documentation sweep once the rollout lands. Update README, AGENTS.md, docs/project-status/STATUS.md, and HISTORY.md so they describe the v0.2 architecture, current phase, and completed rollout accurately.
acceptance_criteria: Governance docs are internally consistent with the landed v0.2 implementation and open/closed bd issues; no stale provider-era public DSL guidance remains.

title: Wire Beads Dolt push to DoltHub repo dlg0/dolt-vedalang

title: Add explanatory comments to example .veda.yaml sections

title: Trade lens blank for dc5_two_regions should report no trade links
description: dc5_two_regions.veda.yaml has multiple regions but no model.trade_links, so query_engine lens=trade returns empty graph (0 nodes/0 edges) with no diagnostics. This looks like a blank-screen bug to users expecting trade in DC5-style examples. Follow-up: (1) add explicit NO_TRADE_LINKS diagnostic for trade lens when graph is empty due missing trade links, and/or (2) align example/docs by adding trade_links to dc5_two_regions or clarifying that example_with_trade/minisystem7-8 are the trade examples.
close_reason: Completed: trade lens now emits NO_TRADE_LINKS warning when model.trade_links is absent and query returns an empty graph; added regression test.

description: Add a stable folder taxonomy under vedalang/examples (without moving existing files yet) so new examples can be grouped, especially component-library-based examples.
close_reason: Completed

title: Move RES file selector to collapsible left sidebar
description: In vedalang viz web app, move file selector into a dedicated full-height left sidebar that is collapsible. Keep other selectors in main content and contextual to selected file.

title: Improve vedalang-dev pipeline run_times failure diagnostics
description: User feedback: pipeline solve failures (licensing/no-solve) are too terse. Include richer GAMS diagnostics in non-JSON output and preserve key details in step artifacts (status codes/text, command, stderr tail, diagnostics path).

close_reason: Implemented canonical .veda.yaml formatting (sorting + spacing) in vedalang fmt with tests

title: Route emission unit diagnostics through deterministic units lint

Keep deterministic behavior explicit and covered by tests.
close_reason: Routed W_NEGATIVE_EMISSION_DOC to units category and added CLI regression coverage.

close_reason: Completed: switched Node tooling workflows/docs to bun commands, updated CLI formatter install hint to bun, replaced root package-lock with bun.lock, and verified formatter/lint/tests.

title: Phase 2: full deterministic parity with llm unit taxonomy

close_reason: Completed: units eval now builds component-scoped deterministic references with deterministic->taxonomy mapping (basis/cost/capacity), and parity scoring now uses error-code overlap when available.

title: Wire formatting checks into quality gates and dev workflow
description: Integrate formatter check into standard quality gates (CI/pre-commit/local commands) so style drift is caught before review. Update contributor guidance with explicit command order (fmt/check -> lint -> validate/tests).
close_reason: Completed: wired format checks into documented quality gates and contributor workflow commands.

title: Document formatter vs linter vs compiler responsibilities
description: Update user and design-agent docs to clarify command boundaries: formatter handles layout/style, lint handles semantic/modeling diagnostics, compiler/validate handle transformation and oracle validation. Include concise CLI examples for each command.
close_reason: Completed: documented formatter vs linter vs compiler command boundaries in user/design docs.

title: Exclude xl2times from Ruff and clear remaining lint issues
description: User-requested repo-wide Ruff cleanup while treating bundled third-party xl2times as excluded from lint scope.
close_reason: Completed

title: Update LSP schema docs/examples to canonical namespace conventions
description: tools/vedalang_lsp/server/schema_docs.py still documents legacy C:/S:/E: commodity naming and TRADABLE/SERVICE/EMISSION kind language. This now conflicts with schema/conventions and lint behavior. Regenerate or rewrite schema docs/examples to show canonical namespaced IDs (primary:/secondary:/service:/emission:/material:/resource:/money) and canonical commodity.type values.
close_reason: Completed: refreshed LSP schema docs/examples to canonical commodity namespaces and commodity.type language; removed legacy C:/S:/E and TRADABLE/SERVICE/EMISSION wording.

title: Migrate remaining C/S/E runtime helpers to canonical commodity namespaces
description: Post-n9c audit found runtime modules still generating legacy C:/S:/E: commodity IDs and TRADABLE/SERVICE/EMISSION semantics (vedalang/compiler/registry_emitter.py, vedalang/viz/graph_builder.py, and legacy identity parser paths). This risks future drift versus schema/conventions and confusing diagnostics. Replace legacy ID construction/parsing with canonical namespace-aware helpers from vedalang.conventions, and either migrate or explicitly quarantine legacy-only pathways.
close_reason: Completed: migrated runtime helpers off legacy C/S/E semantics by canonicalizing registry emitter commodity/process parsing and graph_builder node/edge IDs; added regression coverage.

title: Set llm-lint default candidate and add eval-evolution skill
description: Choose and implement default llm-lint model/reasoning-effort based on calibrated eval outcomes, then add a reusable skill that codifies adding new eval fixtures and evolving prompts when undesirable LLM lint flags are observed.
close_reason: Implemented default llm-lint runtime target (gpt-5-nano:low) and added llm-lint-eval-evolution skill with calibration workflow

title: Eval progress shows label_match as n/a on control cases
description: Progress output used label_match=n/a when intentional_total==0, which obscures whether control labels were actually evaluated. Update scoring/progress to always show [hits/total] for intentional labels (including [0/0]) and add explicit control_match ratio.
close_reason: Implemented explicit [hits/total] label_match plus control_match in scoring/progress output, with tests

title: Track additional unmodeled issues in eval metrics
description: Add explicit additional issue counters for predictions not represented in fixture labels, and make label_match ratios count only intentionally injected (expected_presence=present) issues.
close_reason: Implemented intentional-only match ratios and additional unknown issue counters in eval rows/summaries

title: Add explicit label hit ratios in eval output
description: Expose [hits/total] style known-label match ratios per eval row and per candidate summary; bound deterministic score to <=100 to avoid confusion.
close_reason: Implemented per-row and per-candidate [hits/total] label match output and bounded deterministic score

title: Adjust eval loop ordering and reasoning levels
description: Update vedalang-dev eval to iterate case outermost, reasoning middle, model innermost. Remove none/xhigh reasoning efforts from eval candidate matrix.
close_reason: Implemented case-effort-model eval ordering and removed none/xhigh candidate efforts

title: Reorder eval execution and cache judge responses
description: Run evals in effort->case->model order for easier same-effort model comparison, and cache judge outputs so reruns benefit from cache end-to-end.
close_reason: Implemented new execution order and judge result cache reuse

title: Add bounded parallel execution to vedalang-dev eval run
